items.title: Talks page
items.intent: /talks should only ship when Simon has at least one confirmed or past talk to list. A page with zero talks violates the site's principle that every piece must be grounded in real work — the about page already covers contact and advisory availability.
items.description: Create a talks page listing speaking engagements with topics, dates, and venues. Only build this when there's actual content to ground it. Until then, the about page's advisory section serves the same purpose.
steps: Create src/pages/talks.astro using Base layout with prose container
Write intro text about speaking interests and topic areas (agent infrastructure, AI security, orchestration)
Include contact info for speaking inquiries
Structure the page so past talks can be added later without restructuring

acceptanceCriteria.text: /talks route exists and renders
Page contains contact or inquiry information

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.7: Demoted to future. About page already covers contact/advisory availability. A talks page with zero talks is aspirational filler — violates 'grounded in real work' principle. Revisit when Simon has actual talks to list.

items.title: Reading time calculation
items.intent: Every long-form content page (series, commentary, architecture) must display an estimated reading time calculated from word count — readers decide what to read based on time commitment.
items.description: Create a utility function (words / 200 wpm, round up) and integrate into all three content detail page templates. Reading time should appear in the metadata area near the title/date.
steps: Create a readingTime utility in src/utils/ or inline — input: rendered content string, output: 'N min read'
Integrate into src/pages/series/[slug].astro metadata area
Integrate into src/pages/commentary/[slug].astro metadata area
Integrate into src/pages/architecture/[slug].astro metadata area

acceptanceCriteria.text: Series detail pages include reading time text in built HTML
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && SERIES_PAGE=$(ls dist/series/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head -1) && test -n "$SERIES_PAGE" && grep -qi 'min read\|minute' "$SERIES_PAGE"

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps now done. Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verify: grep built series page for 'min read' text. Added description and specific steps. Utility is trivial — S size. Unblocked and marked ready.

items.title: Product ecosystem relationship map
items.intent: The products overview page must include a visual relationship map showing how ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, and Clawdius fit together in the agent infrastructure lifecycle — so visitors immediately grasp the ecosystem, not just a list of unrelated projects.
items.description: Add a CSS/SVG diagram to the products index page showing product positioning and connections. Must be static (no JS framework), responsive, and accessible. Pure SVG inline or CSS-drawn — no external image files.
steps: Design the relationship diagram layout — products as nodes, connections as lines/arrows showing relationships (e.g., 'AIShore uses ClawHQ', 'Clawdius extends ClawHQ')
Implement as inline SVG or pure CSS in src/pages/products/index.astro or a new EcosystemMap.astro component
Ensure responsive — readable on mobile (stacked layout) and desktop (spatial layout)
Add aria-labels or accessible text alternatives for the diagram
Style to match: dark surfaces, amber accents, DM Serif Display headings

acceptanceCriteria.text: Products page contains an inline SVG diagram element
All four product names appear in the diagram
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -qi 'clawhq' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'aishore' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'easy.markdown\|easymarkdown' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'clawdius' dist/products/index.html

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.7+: Fixed false-positive in AC2. All four product names already exist in products page HTML from FEAT-010 — the old verify would pass without any SVG diagram. Replaced with an aria-label check that's only satisfied when an SVG with 'ecosystem' label exists. Added step requiring aria-label and <title> in the SVG element.

items.title: LLMs.txt generation
items.intent: The site must generate a valid llms.txt file (per llmstxt.org spec) at build time so AI models can discover and understand the site's content, structure, and purpose — this is table stakes for an AI infrastructure site.
items.description: Create a build-time script or Astro endpoint that generates llms.txt from content collections. Include site description, content tracks, key pages, and structured metadata per llmstxt.org spec.
steps: Create src/pages/llms.txt.ts as an Astro static endpoint that outputs text/plain
Query all three content collections (series, commentary, architecture) and products
Structure output per llmstxt.org spec: title, description, sections with URLs and descriptions
Include all published content with titles and URLs
Verify output is valid plain text with correct structure

acceptanceCriteria.text: llms.txt is generated at build time
llms.txt contains site description and content URLs
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -q 'simonplant\|Simon Plant' dist/llms.txt && grep -q 'http\|/' dist/llms.txt

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: added second verify to check llms.txt contains meaningful content (site name + URLs), not just an empty file. Steps updated to be specific — Astro static endpoint approach. Ready.

items.title: Print stylesheet
items.intent: Long-form content must produce clean, readable output when printed or saved as PDF — developers do this constantly with technical articles. Hide nav, footer, and interactive elements. Optimize typography for paper.
items.description: Add @media print styles to global.css. Hide navigation, footer, cookie banner, copy buttons. Optimize line length, font size, and margins for A4/Letter paper. Ensure code blocks don't overflow.
steps: Add @media print block to src/styles/global.css
Hide elements: nav, footer, cookie banner, copy buttons, search overlay — use display: none
Set body background to white, text to black for print
Optimize font size (~12pt), line-height (~1.5), and max-width for A4/Letter width
Ensure code blocks use overflow-wrap: break-word to prevent page overflow
Remove decorative elements (borders, shadows, background colors) in print

acceptanceCriteria.text: @media print styles exist in built CSS
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -rq '@media print' dist/_astro/*.css 2>/dev/null || grep -rq '@media print' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: Print styles hide nav element within @media print block
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -Pzo '@media print[^}]*\{[^}]*display:\s*none' dist/_astro/*.css 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'display'

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.7+: Simplified AC2 verify — previous -Pzo regex only matched if display:none was in the FIRST rule of @media print block. Since steps add multiple rules (body bg, nav, footer, etc.), order is unspecified and the nested brace pattern would fail. New verify: grep the line containing @media print (minified CSS = one line) for 'display'. This works because Vite minifies CSS into a single line, so the entire @media print block is on the matched line.

items.title: PostHog full-scope configuration
items.intent: PostHog must go from a bare pageview counter to a full observability stack — session replay, error tracking, web vitals, heatmaps, dead/rage click detection, and survey capability must all be enabled. The posthog instance must be accessible from any component, not trapped in a closure. Without this, the site has analytics in name only.
items.description: Upgrade posthog.init() configuration in CookieConsent.astro to enable session replay, error tracking ($exception autocapture), web vitals (LCP, FCP, INP, CLS), heatmap data, dead click detection, and survey support. Extract the PostHog instance to a shared reference (window.__posthog or a module-level export) so other components can call capture(). Add privacy masking for session replay (mask all input fields, respect ph-no-capture). Keep consent-gated loading — all features only activate after user accepts.
steps: Update posthog.init() in CookieConsent.astro to add: session_recording: { maskAllInputs: true }, autocapture: true, capture_dead_clicks: true, capture_performance: { web_vitals: true }, enable_heatmaps: true
Store the initialized posthog instance on window (e.g., window.__posthog) so other scripts can access it after consent
Verify session replay, error tracking, and web vitals appear as enabled in PostHog project settings after deployment
Add ph-no-capture attribute to CookieConsent banner and any elements containing email addresses

acceptanceCriteria.text: PostHog init config includes session_recording option
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -q 'session_recording' dist/_astro/*.js 2>/dev/null || grep -rq 'session_recording' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: PostHog init config includes web vitals capture
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -q 'web_vitals\|capture_performance' dist/_astro/*.js 2>/dev/null || grep -rq 'web_vitals\|capture_performance' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: PostHog instance is exposed globally for other scripts
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -q '__posthog\|window.posthog' dist/_astro/*.js 2>/dev/null || grep -rq '__posthog\|window.posthog' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

items.groomingNotes: New item. Current PostHog setup is bare minimum — loads SDK, captures default pageviews, nothing else. This unlocks session replay, error tracking, web vitals, heatmaps, and survey capability with a single-file config change. S size. No deps — CookieConsent.astro already exists. Marked ready.

items.title: Content engagement event instrumentation
items.intent: Simon must know what content actually resonates — not just pageviews, but scroll depth milestones, time-on-article, CTA engagement, search queries, and code copy actions. Without custom events, PostHog is a hit counter. With them, it's a content strategy tool.
items.description: Add PostHog custom event capture calls across content pages and interactive components. Track: article scroll depth milestones (25/50/75/100% via IntersectionObserver), newsletter CTA clicks, GitHub link clicks, search queries entered, code block copy button clicks, and tag filter interactions. All events must be consent-gated (check window.__posthog exists before capturing). Use data-ph-capture-attribute-* on key elements for autocapture enrichment.
steps: Add scroll depth tracking to series/[slug].astro and commentary/[slug].astro — use IntersectionObserver on sentinel elements at 25/50/75/100% of .prose container, capture 'article_scroll_depth' with properties: slug, depth_percent, title
Add capture call to CopyCode.astro copy button click — capture 'code_block_copied' with page slug
Add capture call to SearchOverlay.astro on search submit — capture 'search_query' with query text and result count
Add capture call to newsletter CTA clicks — capture 'newsletter_cta_clicked' with source page
Add data-ph-capture-attribute-content-type and data-ph-capture-attribute-slug attributes to content page containers for autocapture enrichment
Ensure all capture calls are gated on window.__posthog existence (no errors if consent not given)

acceptanceCriteria.text: Scroll depth tracking code exists in built series detail pages
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && SERIES_PAGE=$(ls dist/series/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head -1) && test -n "$SERIES_PAGE" && grep -qi 'scroll_depth\|IntersectionObserver' "$SERIES_PAGE"

acceptanceCriteria.text: Code copy tracking exists in built output
Search query tracking exists in built output
Build succeeds cleanly

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.7+: Fixed AC1 verify — scroll depth script added to series/[slug].astro will be bundled by Astro into dist/_astro/*.js, not inlined into the series HTML page. Changed verify from grep on HTML file to grep -r dist/ which catches both bundled and inline locations. AC2/AC3 already use -r dist/ so those are correct. Depends on FEAT-033 (window.__posthog). M size, ready when FEAT-033 ships.

items.title: PostHog reverse proxy via Cloudflare
items.intent: PostHog traffic must route through the site's own domain so ad blockers and privacy extensions don't block analytics — typically recovers 10-30% of lost events. An AI infrastructure site that can't measure its own audience is ironic.
items.description: Configure a Cloudflare Worker or Pages Function to proxy PostHog API requests through a first-party subdomain (e.g., e.simonplant.com or /ingest/ path rewrite). Update the api_host in posthog.init() to use the proxy endpoint instead of us.i.posthog.com. Ensure the proxy forwards all required PostHog endpoints (/e/, /decide/, /engage/, static assets).
steps: Create a Cloudflare Pages Function or Worker at /ingest/* that proxies requests to us.i.posthog.com, rewriting the path and forwarding headers
Proxy must handle: POST /ingest/e/ (events), POST /ingest/decide/ (feature flags), GET /ingest/array/* (JS bundle), POST /ingest/engage/ (surveys)
Update api_host in CookieConsent.astro posthog.init() to use the proxy path (e.g., api_host: '/ingest')
Test that events still arrive in PostHog dashboard via the proxied path
Document the proxy setup in a code comment for future reference

acceptanceCriteria.text: PostHog api_host points to first-party proxy, not us.i.posthog.com
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && ! grep -q 'us.i.posthog.com' dist/_astro/*.js 2>/dev/null && ! grep -rq 'us.i.posthog.com' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: Proxy route or worker file exists
acceptanceCriteria.verify: test -f functions/ingest/\[\[path\]\].ts || test -f functions/ingest.ts || test -f workers/posthog-proxy.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds cleanly

items.groomingNotes: New item. Depends on FEAT-033 (PostHog config). Cloudflare Pages Functions can act as reverse proxy with zero additional infrastructure. S size — one proxy function + config change. Note: first AC verify may need adjustment if env var still defaults to us.i.posthog.com as fallback. Marked ready (blocked by FEAT-033).

items.title: PostHog analytics dashboards and conversion goals
items.intent: Simon must have dashboards that answer the three questions that matter: what content is working, where do visitors come from, and what do they do after reading. Conversion goals must define success in measurable terms — newsletter CTA clicks, article completions, contact link clicks — so the content strategy has feedback, not just vibes.
items.description:
Create PostHog dashboards and conversion goals via PostHog UI. No code changes required — this is configuration work in the PostHog web app after FEAT-033 and FEAT-034 are deployed.

Dashboards to create:
1. Web Analytics Overview — visitors, sessions, bounce rate, top pages, traffic sources, geographic distribution
2. Content Performance — pageviews by article, scroll depth completion rates, time on page, top tags, series vs commentary vs architecture engagement
3. Engagement Funnel — landing page → article read → scroll 50%+ → newsletter CTA click

Conversion goals to define:
- newsletter_cta_clicked (from FEAT-034 custom event)
- article_scroll_depth at 100% (article completion)
- Outbound click to GitHub product repos
steps: [PostHog UI] Create 'Web Analytics Overview' dashboard with: visitors trend, top pages by views, traffic sources, referrers, device/browser breakdown, geographic map

[PostHog UI] Create 'Content Performance' dashboard with: pageviews by slug, scroll depth distribution, average time on article pages, top content by completion rate, content type comparison (series vs commentary vs architecture)
[PostHog UI] Create 'Engagement Funnel' insight: pageview → article_scroll_depth(50%) → article_scroll_depth(100%) → newsletter_cta_clicked
[PostHog UI] Define conversion goals: newsletter_cta_clicked, article_scroll_depth(100%), outbound GitHub clicks
[PostHog UI] Set up weekly email digest of key metrics
acceptanceCriteria: Web Analytics Overview dashboard exists in PostHog with visitor trends and traffic sources
Content Performance dashboard exists with per-article engagement metrics
At least one conversion goal is defined and tracking
Engagement funnel shows drop-off between read stages
items.groomingNotes: New item. simon/ui — PostHog dashboard configuration, no code changes. Blocked by FEAT-033 (features enabled) and FEAT-034 (custom events exist). Cannot be automated — requires manual PostHog UI work. S size in effort, high value.

items.title: PostHog reader feedback surveys
items.intent: Readers who finish an article must have a low-friction way to signal whether it was useful — qualitative feedback is the only way to calibrate content quality beyond scroll metrics. Surveys must be unobtrusive, targeted to engaged readers, and yield actionable signal.
items.description:
Configure PostHog popover surveys via PostHog UI. No code changes required — surveys are rendered by the PostHog SDK once enabled in FEAT-033.

Surveys to configure:
1. Article feedback (triggered after 75%+ scroll on series/commentary pages): 'Was this useful?' rating (1-5) + optional freeform 'What would make this better?'
2. Site feedback (shown to returning visitors after 3+ sessions): 'What topic should Simon write about next?' freeform

Targeting: URL contains /series/ or /commentary/, minimum scroll depth, wait period between surveys (30 days).
steps: [PostHog UI] Create 'Article Feedback' survey: rating question (1-5 emoji) + conditional freeform follow-up for ratings <= 3
[PostHog UI] Set targeting: URL matches /series/* or /commentary/*, show after article_scroll_depth(75%) event, desktop and mobile
[PostHog UI] Set wait period: don't show to users who completed any survey in last 30 days
[PostHog UI] Create 'Topic Suggestion' survey: single freeform question, target returning visitors (3+ sessions)
[PostHog UI] Style surveys to match site design: dark background, amber accent, clean typography
acceptanceCriteria: Article Feedback survey is live and triggers on content pages after scroll engagement
Topic Suggestion survey targets returning visitors only
Survey styling is consistent with site dark theme
items.groomingNotes: New item. simon/ui — PostHog survey configuration, no code changes. Blocked by FEAT-033 (survey capability enabled in SDK). Popover surveys are rendered by posthog-js — zero component changes needed. Targeting by URL and events. XS effort, high qualitative value.

items.title: PostHog session replay and heatmap review workflow
items.intent: Session replays and heatmaps are useless if nobody watches them. Simon must establish a weekly review cadence — watch 5-10 replays of engaged readers, review scroll heatmaps on top content pages, and identify UX friction points. The first review must produce at least one actionable finding.
items.description:
Set up session replay filters and heatmap views in PostHog UI. No code changes required — session replay and heatmap data collection is enabled by FEAT-033.

Setup tasks:
1. Create saved session replay filters: 'Engaged readers' (sessions with article_scroll_depth 75%+), 'Frustrated users' (sessions with rage clicks or dead clicks), 'Search users' (sessions with search_query event)
2. Review scroll heatmaps on top 5 content pages — note where readers drop off
3. Review click heatmaps on home page and products page — identify missed click targets
4. Document findings and create bug/improvement items from observations
steps: [PostHog UI] Create saved replay filter: 'Engaged readers' — sessions containing article_scroll_depth event with depth >= 75
[PostHog UI] Create saved replay filter: 'Frustrated users' — sessions with rage_click or dead_click events
[PostHog UI] Create saved replay filter: 'Search users' — sessions containing search_query event
[PostHog UI] Review scroll heatmaps on top 5 pages by traffic, note drop-off points
[PostHog UI] Review click heatmaps on home page and products index, identify dead click zones
[PostHog UI] Document at least one actionable finding as a new backlog item or bug
acceptanceCriteria: Three saved session replay filters exist in PostHog (engaged, frustrated, search)
Scroll heatmaps reviewed for top 5 content pages
At least one actionable finding documented as a backlog item or bug
items.groomingNotes: New item. simon/ui — PostHog replay and heatmap setup + first review cycle. Blocked by FEAT-033 + FEAT-034 (needs replay data and custom events). This is the 'close the loop' item — data collection without review is waste. S effort.

items.title: PostHog no-code A/B experiments
items.intent: The site's newsletter CTA, home page hero copy, and content page layout assumptions must be tested, not guessed. PostHog no-code experiments let Simon test visual changes without code deploys — run at least one experiment to validate the workflow and establish a testing habit.
items.description:
Set up and run at least one no-code A/B experiment via PostHog UI toolbar. No code changes required — PostHog's no-code experiments modify DOM elements client-side based on feature flag assignment.

Candidate experiments (pick one to start):
1. Newsletter CTA copy: 'Subscribe' vs 'Get the next installment' vs 'Join 100+ builders'
2. Home page hero tagline: test current copy vs a more specific variant
3. Series landing page: test showing reading time vs not (if FEAT-017 ships first)

Success metric: newsletter_cta_clicked conversion rate (or relevant engagement metric).
steps: [PostHog UI] Choose first experiment — newsletter CTA copy is recommended (clear conversion metric, high traffic element)
[PostHog UI] Use PostHog toolbar to define control and 1-2 variants with modified text/styling
[PostHog UI] Set primary metric: newsletter_cta_clicked conversion rate
[PostHog UI] Set traffic allocation: 50/50 or 33/33/33 split
[PostHog UI] Run experiment for minimum 2 weeks or until statistical significance
[PostHog UI] Review results, implement winning variant permanently (this step may require a code PR)
acceptanceCriteria: At least one A/B experiment has been created and launched in PostHog
Experiment has a defined primary metric tied to a real conversion goal
Experiment runs for minimum 2 weeks before concluding
items.groomingNotes: New item. simon/ui — PostHog no-code experiment. Blocked by FEAT-033 + FEAT-034 (needs SDK features enabled and custom events for metrics). First experiment establishes the testing workflow. XS effort to set up, requires patience to run. Could priority — valuable but not urgent.

items.title: robots.txt
items.intent: The site must serve a valid robots.txt at /robots.txt that allows all crawlers and references the XML sitemap — search engines and AI scrapers need this to correctly index the site.
items.description: Create a static robots.txt file in public/ that allows all crawlers (User-agent: *) and includes the Sitemap directive pointing to the site's sitemap.xml. Required basic SEO infrastructure missing from the site.
steps: Create public/robots.txt with 'User-agent: *' and 'Allow: /' directives
Add 'Sitemap: https://www.simonplant.com/sitemap-index.xml' directive to the file
Verify the file appears at dist/robots.txt after build

acceptanceCriteria.text: robots.txt exists in built output
robots.txt references the sitemap
robots.txt allows all crawlers

items.groomingNotes: New item. Static file in public/robots.txt — trivial implementation. XS size. Scope: public/robots.txt only. No dependencies. Unblocked and marked ready.
